2606.11744.title: Hey Chat, Can You Teach Me? Structuring Socratic Dialogue for Human Learning in the Wild
2606.11744.paper_summary: Large language models are now widely used for everyday learning, but the underlying interactions are typically unstructured chats rather than following a curriculum. Unlike formal online learning systems, these interactions carry no prior record of the student, so any estimate of what the student already knows must be inferred from the dialogue itself. We show that this gap is not closed by scaling models alone. Frontier and education-tuned LLMs perform poorly when asked to tutor a student over an extended session, because doing so requires three things at once. The tutor must sequence a curriculum, conduct Socratic dialogue, and infer the student's knowledge state from that dialogue. We propose separating these responsibilities. Given a student query, our system constructs a prerequisite knowledge graph in which subtopics are nodes and dependencies are edges, and frames tutoring as deciding which node to teach next and how many dialogue turns to spend on it before moving on. A lightweight PPO policy handles this sequencing decision, while an LLM conducts the Socratic exchange at the chosen node and returns a signal of student progress. Across held-out STEM and non-STEM topics, our PPO-paired tutor outperforms heuristic baselines, frontier general-purpose models, and a model specialised for Socratic dialogue: on both the rate at which students reach full curriculum mastery and the number of turns required. Explicit curriculum structure delivers gains that scaling the underlying model does not.
2606.11744.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型現在被廣泛用於日常學習，但其背後的互動通常是非結構化的對話，而不是遵循課程。與正式的在線學習系統不同，這些互動沒有學生的先前記錄，因此對學生已經知道的內容的任何估計都必須從對話本身推斷。我們顯示僅僅擴大模型並不能填補這一空白。當被要求在長時間的會話中輔導學生時，前沿和教育調整的LLM表現不佳，因為這樣做同時需要三件事情。輔導者必須安排課程，進行蘇格拉底式對話，並從該對話中推斷學生的知識狀態。我們建議將這些責任分開。根據學生的查詢，我們的系統構建一個先決知識圖，其中子主題是節點，依賴關係是邊，並將輔導框架化為決定下一個教學的節點以及在轉移之前要花多少對話輪次。輕量級的PPO策略處理這一排序決策，而LLM則在選定的節點進行蘇格拉底式交流並返回學生進展的信號。在保留的STEM和非STEM主題中，我們的PPO配對輔導者在學生達到完整課程掌握的速度和所需的輪次上表現優於啟發式基準、前沿通用模型以及專門針對蘇格拉底對話的模型：明確的課程結構帶來的收益是擴大基礎模型所無法實現的。
2606.11744.author: Sidney Tio et.al.
2606.11744.authors: Sidney Tio, Arunesh Sinha, Pradeep Varakantham
